assertion.title: Adding transcendental operators and linear scaling improves exact matches from 17/50 to 24/50
assertion.statement: Adding square, sqrt, sin, cos, exp, log unary operators plus linear scaling (lstsq-based) to fitness and final evaluation improves exact match count from 17/50 (baseline) to 24/50 (+41%). Tier 1 goes from 7/10 to 10/10 (perfect), Tier 2 from 8/12 to 9/12, Tier 3 from 2/10 to 4/10, Tier 4 from 0/18 to 1/18. The square operator is critical for quadratic physics formulas (KE, Spring PE, Coulomb, etc). Linear scaling separates constant-finding from structure-finding, letting the GP focus on functional form.

key_new_solves.eq05_distance: t*(v + v + t*a) with linear scaling

changes_made: Added 6 unary operators: square, sqrt, sin, cos, exp, log (with safe guards)
Added linear scaling via numpy lstsq in fitness() and run_equation()
Increased unary selection probability from 0.2 to 0.3
